---
title: "About This Certificate"
---

## The Graduate Research Core Competencies Certificate

The **Graduate Research Core Competencies Certificate** is a self-paced online program developed by the **UC Riverside Library** to support graduate students at all stages of their research journey.

### Program Goals

By completing this certificate, graduate students gain practical, transferable skills to:

- Conduct, manage, and disseminate their research **ethically and efficiently**
- Increase their **research visibility** and professional profile
- Meet expectations of **funding agencies and academic publishers**
- Navigate UCR's **graduation requirements** with confidence

### Who This Is For

This certificate is designed for **all UCR graduate students**, regardless of discipline or career stage — whether you are just starting your program or preparing to file your dissertation.

---

## Program Structure

The certificate consists of **seven modules** that can be completed in any order:

| Module | Topic |
|---|---|
| 1 | Citation Management |
| 2 | Scholarly Communication |
| 3 | Data Management |
| 4 | Literature Review Strategies |
| 5 | Publish & Share Your Research |
| 6 | Graduate Academic Affairs |
| 7 (Capstone) | Research Plan |

A certificate is awarded upon completion of all modules and the capstone assignment.

---

## Developed By

This certificate program was developed by the **UC Riverside Library** in partnership with the **UCR Graduate Division**.

**Key contributors:**

- **Jing Han** — Digital Scholarship Librarian · [jingh@ucr.edu](mailto:jingh@ucr.edu)
- **Dr. Barbara Martinez Neda** — Research Data Scientist · [barbara.martinezneda@ucr.edu](mailto:barbara.martinezneda@ucr.edu)
- **Mary-Michelle Moore** — STEM Teaching Librarian · [marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu](mailto:marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu)
- **Michele Potter** — STEM Collection Strategist

**Library website:** [library.ucr.edu](https://library.ucr.edu)

---

## Technical Notes

This site is built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) and hosted on [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com). The source course was originally developed in Canvas LMS and exported in IMS Common Cartridge (`.imscc`) format.

To report issues or suggest improvements, please open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/grcc-certificate).
